steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank precedes an appositive phrase — "the cellular organs that generate energy from light" — that renames the noun before it. After the appositive, an em-dash appears before "reshuffled." The question tests punctuation consistency around an appositive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "chloroplasts—": the appositive ends with an em-dash, so it must open with one. Matching em-dashes correctly set off the appositive on both sides. ✓
  • "chloroplasts,": mixing a comma opener with an em-dash closer is inconsistent punctuation. ✗
  • "chloroplasts": leaves the appositive without opening punctuation, so it does not match the closing em-dash before "reshuffled." ✗
  • "chloroplasts;": semicolons separate independent clauses, not appositives, and a semicolon cannot open a paired appositive before the closing em-dash. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
